description: Standalone command that animates stroke and/or fill properties of existing shapes. Applies highlight styling to individual shapes — for filling regions between shapes, use highlightfill (2D) or highlightfill3d (3D). For dashed outline highlights, use highlightdash. Takes shape variable references followed by any combination of style modifiers: c() for stroke color, fc() for fill color, so() for stroke opacity, fo() for fill opacity, s() for stroke width. Auto-clears after t() duration (default 5 seconds).
utility: Animate stroke color, fill color, opacity, or width change on existing shapes

arguments.name: shapes
arguments.description: One or more variable references to existing shapes - e.g., A, B, L

arguments.name: strokeColor
arguments.description: Stroke color via c() - e.g., c(red), c("#ff0000")

arguments.name: fillColor
arguments.description: Fill color via fc() - e.g., fc(blue), fc("#0000ff")

arguments.name: strokeOpacity
arguments.description: Stroke opacity from 0 (transparent) to 1 (opaque) via so()

arguments.name: fillOpacity
arguments.description: Fill opacity from 0 (transparent) to 1 (opaque) via fo()

arguments.name: strokeWidth
arguments.description: Stroke width in pixels via s()

arguments.name: duration
arguments.description: Auto-clear duration. t(0) = permanent. Defaults to 5 seconds.

alternates.description: Change stroke color of a shape
Change fill color of a shape
Change both stroke and fill color at once
Change stroke color with 50% stroke opacity
Change fill color with 50% fill opacity
Stroke color + fill color + fill opacity together
Change only stroke opacity without changing color
Change only fill opacity without changing color
Change stroke width of a shape
Apply highlight to multiple shapes at once

examples.expression: effect_1 = highlight(select_1, line_1, vector_1, c(red), t(1))
stroke_line_c=highlight(line_c, s(8), notes("For this triangle We will treat this as base"))

examples.scenario: Highlight a triangle with yellow fill
Highlight selected discriminant, line and vector in quadratic formula with timed auto-clear
examples.expression:
select_1 = select(write_1, "sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)", 1)
effect_1 = highlight(select_1, line_1, vector_1, c(red), t(1))

examples.scenario: Highlight two points across separate graphs with color and timer
